---
title: "Yang Puhan"
subtitle: "Fintech 533 — course site and notes"
---

::: {.hero-lead}
Hi — I’m **Yang Puhan**. This is my Quarto site for **Fintech 533**: assignments, write-ups, and links to my GitHub repo below.
:::

![Site banner with initials YP](images/banner.svg){fig-alt="Banner graphic with initials Y P and Fintech 533" fig-align="center" width="560"}

## What you get

::: {.row .g-4 .my-4}

::: {.col-md-4}
::: {.feature-card}
### Projects

Render the whole folder with one command. Shared YAML keeps titles, theme, and output in sync.
:::
:::

::: {.col-md-4}
::: {.feature-card}
### Authoring

Write in Markdown (`.qmd`) or Jupyter (`.ipynb`). Quarto turns them into HTML, PDF, and more.
:::
:::

::: {.col-md-4}
::: {.feature-card}
### Polished HTML

Bootstrap-based layout, table of contents, and search — suitable for assignments and portfolios.
:::
:::

:::

## Quick start

::: {.section-muted}
From this project folder, update the site and publish:

```bash
quarto render
git add .
git commit -m "Update site"
git push
```

GitHub Pages serves the **`docs/`** folder on branch **`main`**.
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Tip
Replace the banner in `images/banner.svg` with your own photo: save it as e.g. `images/photo.jpg` and update the image line above to `![](images/photo.jpg)`.
:::
